reasoning: The informal statement asks for a counterexample: exhibit a connected set S whose interior is disconnected (classically in ℝ²). The corrected formalization fixes S : Set (ℝ × ℝ), providing such a counterexample. The original, however, makes X an implicit universally quantified type variable: ∀ (X : Type*) [TopologicalSpace X], ∃ S : Set X, IsConnected S ∧ ¬ IsConnected (interior S). This is strictly stronger than the informal claim — it demands a counterexample in EVERY topological space. This is provably false: take X = Unit (singleton space). The only nonempty set is {()}, which is connected, and its interior is itself (connected), so no S satisfies the existential. Hence the original is stronger than the informal and provably false. The error is adding an extra universal quantification over X, which introduces cases (like singleton/empty spaces) that are not part of the informal statement.
